title: Video Storyboard Document
summary: Watch a short video and create a factual DOCX storyboard that preserves visual details needed to answer a later question set.
taskPrompt:
You are creating a storyboard document for a short video on a Windows VM.

## Input Files
- Video: `input/Yasuji_Murata-_A_Wolf_is_a_Wolf_(1931).ogv`
- Question set: `input/video_storyboard_qa_set.docx`

## Software
Use any DOCX-capable workflow available on the VM. A task-local launcher is available at `software/launch_word_processor.cmd`.

## Your Task
1. Watch or inspect the full input video.
2. Read the question set so your storyboard preserves facts needed for later answering.
3. Create a concise, complete storyboard in temporal order.
4. For each shot or segment, include shot/segment ID, start and end time, visual description, key actions, important objects/people/scene details, and visible text or dialogue needed for the questions.
5. Do not answer the questions directly. Use only information grounded in the video.

## Output
Save the final readable Word document exactly as `output/storyboard.docx`.
agentMustDo: Use the question set to identify details the storyboard must preserve.
Write a temporal storyboard with segment IDs, times, visual descriptions, actions, objects, people, and relevant visible text or dialogue.
Save a valid DOCX file named `storyboard.docx`.

inputFiles.name: video_storyboard_qa_set.docx
inputFiles.description: Question set that defines which factual details the storyboard must preserve.

outputContracts.name: Storyboard document requirements
outputContracts.description: The storyboard must be temporal, grounded in the video, and include the required shot or segment details.

evaluation: The evaluator reads the submitted DOCX and scores whether it is valid, grounded in the video, temporally organized, and sufficiently complete to support the question set without directly answering it.

classifier.rationale: The task involves creating a storyboard for a video, which falls under visual media arts.
